chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT LARKANA Present: Shamsuddin Abbasi, J. Agha Faisal, J. C.P D-433 of 2018: Aijaz Hussain vs. Province of Sindh and others. For the Petitioner: In person. For Private Respondent: Mr. Abdul Wajid Khokhar, Advocate For Official Respondents: Mr. Abdul Hamid Bhurgri, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Date of hearing: 15.05.2024 Date of Order: 15.05.2024 ORDER Agha Faisal, J. The petitioners seek orders of this Court appointing them as primary school teachers. No infirmity, in the recruitment process impugned, was even endeavored to be demonstrated before the Court and the entire pleadings pivoted upon the averment that the petitioners ought to have been considered instead. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the plea raised is factual in nature and the writ jurisdiction is not the appropriate forum in such regard. There was also no endeavor to demonstrate the existence of any vested right in respect of the petitioners to maintain this petition. No case has been set forth before us to merit the invocation of the discretionary writ jurisdiction of this Court in such regard; therefore, this petition is hereby dismissed. Judge Judge 2016 CLC 1; 2015 PLC 45; 2015 CLD 257; 2011 SCMR 1990; 2001 SCMR 574; PLD 2001 Supreme Court 415. Per Ijaz Ul Ahsan J. in Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
